content.full_text:
Fern PolrrrcAl PnecrrcEs CoMMrssroN
-128 J Street . Suite 620 e Sacranrenro. CA 9,5g1-l-l-319
(916) -312--5660 o Fax (916) -112-0886
September 2,2008
Marguerite Lawry,
Bethel Island Municipal
Improvement District
P.O. Box 429
Bethel Island, California 945 I l-0429
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-128
Dear Ms. Lawry:
This letter responds to your reqllest fbr advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of'the Political Reform Act (the "Act")l and is based on the fhcts presente4;
thc- Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of
fact when it renders advice. (ln re oglesbv ( I q75) l Fppc ops. 7 I .) Adclitionally, our
advice is limited to obligations arising under the Act. We do not address the applitability,
if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interesi or
Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTION
Do you have an irnpermissible conflict of interest that would prevent you from
participating in a decision re-uarding a license agreement between the Bethel island
Mr,rnicipal Improvement District and the pleasantimes water cornpany,l
CONCLUSION
No. Because yoLl do not ltave any economic interests that will be financially
affected by the governmental decision, you do not have a conflict of interest.
' The Rllitical Retirrm Act is contained in GoVernment Cocle Sections lil0(X) through 9l0l-1. All
statut()lY l'cf'erences are t() the Gol'c'rnntent Code'. unless othc.rwise indicuted. 1'he regulati.is.f the Fair
Ptl I it ica I Pract ices Contnt ission aru' e rrnta ined in Sect ions I ti I I 0 through I It997 ttf Title I of the Cal itirr.nia
Coclc of Regulations. All regulatoly ref'erence's ale to'fille l. Dilisiori 6 of the Calitirrnia Code of
Re-uulations. unless rltherwise indicated.


File No. A-08-128
Page No. 2
FACTS2
You are a boardmember of the Bethel Island Municipal Improvement District
("BIMID"). You own a home on a lot in the Pleasantimes subdivision of Bethel tsland.
Until Jtrly 2007 yov received residential water service from the pleasantimes Water
Company ("Pleasantimes"). As a result of changes described below, in July 2007 you
became a member of the Sand Mound Water Company, and since that time you have
received domestic water service from sand Mound water company.
This change in water companies was the result of construction of a major
subdivision in the center of Bethel Island, adjacent to your home, called Delta'Coves.
The Delta Coves consists of newly constructed levees and deep water access to
Sandmound Slough through a breach in the existing levee. ThL breech is approximately
150 feet wide and the water is 12 to 35 feet deep. io seclrre the breach, foui rows of
interlocking sheet piles were driven approximately 60 feet deep on each side of the new
breach and through the existing levee.
Stone Road now ends at each side of the breach as do all utility lines, including
the water lines. Eleven homes at the end of the Pleasantimes water lines, which includes
your home, were permanently cut off from water service and were transferred to Sand
Mound Water. Although you no longer receive water from Pleasantimes, you remain a
member3 of the company, as your loicontinues to be in the Pleasantimes subdivision,
which, except for the eleven homes specified above, pleasantimes services.
The governmental decision in question would grant a license or other permission
for Pleasantimes to keep certain water lines and facilities within real property'owneo by
BIMID. Pleasantimes placed the facilities within BIMID's property 6y mistake and
would like permission to leave the facilities in place for an e*t"rd"i period of time
!9fore the necessity of removing the lines. If pirmission is not granted, pleasantimes will
likely incur certain expenses in relocating the iines and may hav-e to recoup those costs in
the form of increased rates to its customers.
ANALYSIS
PornNrrlI. CoNFLrcr oF INTEREsT
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensllre that public officials will ..perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported th;." (Section gl00l(b).)
Section 87100 prohibits any public official frorn-making, parricipating in making, or
I You provided further intbrmation. included herein, in our telephone conversation of
August lO. l00tl.
I According tt) a letter you provided fiom your attorney. and infbrmation you provided in our
telephone conversation, 'srtpra, the term "member'ls defined by pleasantimes to incluje ,ny prop.uy
owner within the Pleasantimes subdivision serviced by the water company.


File No. A-08-128
Page No.3
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regularion f SZOO(U).) The general
ntle, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes a
governmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one
or more of his or her financial interests.
Srnps I & 2z Ann You A Punltc Orrtcr.lr, MaxrNG, plnucrparrNc rN Marnc, oR
INrlunNcrNG A GovERNMENTAL DrcrcroN?
Govemment Code Section 82048 defines "public official" as every member,
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local govemment agency. As a member of
BIMID, you are a public official under the Act. (Section 82048:) Consequently, you may
not make, participate in making, or otherwise use your official position to influence any
decisions that will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on any of your
economic interests. As you will be called upon to consider whether BIMID should upprou"
or disapprove a license agreement with the Fleasantimes Water Company you will be
making, participating in making, or otherwise using your officiat positio, to influence a
governmental decision.
SrBp 3: Do You Havr e PoTnNTTALLY DrsgueImYING ECoNoMIC INrBnrsr?
A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of
section 87103 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect' distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a
member of his or her immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated economic
interests, including:
o An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or indirect
investment of $2,000 or more (section g7103(a); reg. lg703.l(a)); or in which he or
she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee,-or holds any position of
management (Section 87 103(d); Regularion 1 g703. l(b));
o An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(b); Regulation 1g703.2):
o An economic interest in any source of income, including promised income,
aggregating $500 or more within l2 months prior to the dLcision (Section tizto:(");
Regulation 18703.3);
o An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts aggregate to $390
or more within l2 months prior to the decision (Section 87103(I); nelitaion
18703.4);


File No. A-08-128
Page No.4
o An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his or her
immediate family -- this is the "personal financial effects,, rule
(Section 87103; Regularion 18703.5).
Nothing in your facts indicates that you have an economic interest in any business
entity or in any person as a soLlrce of income of as a source of a gift. Although you are a
"member" of the entity involved in the governmental decision, the term *"rrib". simply
means that you live within a certain geographical area and that you may receive services
by virtue of living in that area. Accordingly, you do not have an economic interest in
Pleasantimes.
You do have an economic interest in your personal finances and in the real
property that you o*n.o Therefore, our analysis is limited to those interests.
SrBp 4: Is Tur EcoNourtc Iurrnnsr Dmncrly oR INDTRECTLy lNvor,vso rN THE
GovnmvuENTAL Drclstotrl?
"[n order to determine if a governmental decision's reasonably foreseeable
financial effect on a given economic interest is material, it must first be determined if the
official's economic interest is directly involved or indirectly involved in the
governmental decision. " (Regulation I g704(a). )
Real Prooertv:
For govemmental decisions that affect real property interests, the standards set
forth in regulation 18704.2 apply. (Regulation l87oa(a(i).) For governmental decisions
that affect business entities, the standards set forth in regulatio n tt:lOqJ apply.
(Regulation I 870a(a)(l).)
Regulation lB7 O4.Z(a) srares:
"(a) Real property in which a pubric official has an economic
interest is directly involved in a governmental decision if any of the
following apply:
"( I ) The real property in which the official has an interest, or any
part of that real property, is located within 500 feet of the boundaries (or
the proposed.boundaries) of rhe property [that] is the subject of the
govemmental decision. "
"(2) The governmentar decision involves the zoning or rezoning,
annexation or deannexation, sale, purchase, or rease, or inciusion in or
exclusion fiom any city, county, district or other local governmental
+ Although you have not indicated a value, we think we can reasonably assume that your property
is valued at more than $2.000.


File No. A-08-128
Page No. 5
subdivision, of the real property in which the official has an interest or a
similar decision affecting the real property. For purposes of this subdivision,
the terms "zoning" and "rezoning" shall ref'er toihe act of establishing or
changing the zoning or land use designation on the real property in *"hich the
official has an interest.
"(3) The governmental decision involves the issuance, denial or
revocation of a license, permit or other land use entitlement authorizing a
specific use or uses of the real property in which the official, has an inf,rest.
"(4) The governmental decision involves the imposition, repeal or
modification of any taxes or fees assessed or imposeO on the real pioperty in
which the official has an interest.
"(5) The governmental decision is to designate the survey area, to
select the project area, to adopt the preliminary plan, to form a pioject area
committee, to certify the environmental document, to adopt the
redevelopment plan, to add territory to the redevelopment area, or to rescind
or amend any of the above decisions; and real prop"rty in which the official
has an interest, or any part of it is located within the boundaries (or the
proposed boundaries) of the redevelopment area.
' 
"(6) The decision involves construction of, or improvements to,
streets, water, sewer, storm drainage or similar facilities, and the real property
in which the official has an interest will receive new or improved ,".ui."r.,,
Because your property does not meet any of the criteria under Regulation
18704.2, your property is not directly involved in the decision.
Personal Finances
Under the facts presented, there is nothing to indicate that your personal finances
will be affected, as you now receive you water services from Sand Mound Water
99mpany, so that you will not be affected by any possible rate increases imposed by
Pleasantimes, whether or not the increase isieasonably foreseeable or material.
Accordingly, because there is no indication that there will be any financial effect on your
personal finances as a result of this decision, your personal finances are not directly
involved.
SrBp5: Mlrnnralrry STANDARD
A conflict of interest arises only when the reasonably foreseeable financial effect
of a governmental decision on a public official's economic interest is material.
(Regulation 18700(a).).When real property is not directly involved in a govemmental
decision, it is deemed "indirectly involved;' for purposes tf apptying the materiality
srandard. (Regulation tB704.z (dx2) and Regulario; l8705.2tbl.l
For real property indirectly involved in a governmental decision, the flnancial
effect of a governmental decision on the real proferty is presumed not to be material.


File No. A-08-128
Page No. 6
This presumption may be rebutted under certain factors set forth in Regr-rlation 1g705.2,
none of which are applicable here. Likewise, because there will be no financial effect on
your personal finances as a result of the decision, the materiality standard of $250 has not
been met.
Therefore, because the govemmental decision will not have any financial effect
on you economic interests, our analysis ends at step 5, and you do not 
-have 
a conflict of
interest in participating in the governmental decision descriLed.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Will
Lenkeit
Senior
Legal Division
WJL:jgl
